title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
description: When you edit your Taste Profile, you'll impact your personalized playlists like Discover Weekly, recommendations, and Wrapped.

title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
description: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the largest venture-backed acquisition in history — Google’s $32 billion deal, finalized after a declined 2024 offer, antitrust review on both sides of the Atlantic, and an […]

title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
description: Peacock is betting on new AI-powered video experiences, vertical clips, and mobile games to help its growth.

title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
description: After selling his AI startup to AMD for $665 million, Peter Sarlin is back with Qutwo, a new venture building the infrastructure it believes enterprises will need when quantum computing finally arrives.

title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
description: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a call on their behalf if they suspect a family member might get scammed.

title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
description: GTC — which stands for GPU Technology Conference — is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically uses the spotlight to announce new products, champion partnerships, and lay out its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of computing and AI.

title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
description: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

title: Bumble introduces an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
description: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
description: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
description: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an example of the kind of tool that will unlock that potential.

title: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t do NSFW content
description: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.
